task_completion.description: Load the input polygon GeoJSON file into QGIS
task_completion.visual_evidence: The QGIS Layers panel shows 'sample_polygon' loaded, and polygon features are visible on the map canvas.

task_completion.description: Extract the vertices from the polygon layer into a new point layer
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new point layer (e.g., 'Vertices' or 'Extracted vertices') is visible in the Layers panel, and point features are displayed at the corners of the polygons on the map canvas.

task_completion.description: Add explicit X (longitude) and Y (latitude) coordinate columns to the point layer
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Attribute Table for the point layer is open, showing newly created columns for X and Y coordinates populated with decimal values, or the Field Calculator dialog is visible configuring these fields.

task_completion.description: Export the point layer as a CSV file to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Save Vector Layer as...' dialog is visible with the format set to CSV and the File name set to '/home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/polygon_vertices.csv', or a terminal/file manager shows this file was created.

task_completion.description: Ensure the exported CSV contains all required attribute columns
task_completion.visual_evidence: The export dialog shows the fields selected for export, OR the final CSV is opened in a text editor/terminal, clearly displaying columns for X, Y, vertex_index, name, and id.

task_completion.description: Save the QGIS project to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: The QGIS window title bar displays 'vertex_extraction' indicating the project is saved, or the 'Save Project' dialog is visible with the path '/home/ga/GIS_Data/projects/vertex_extraction.qgs'.

integrity.description: The agent used the QGIS graphical interface for GIS operations rather than bypassing it with terminal scripts
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with QGIS menus, Field Calculator, and processing toolboxes (like 'Extract vertices') rather than executing GDAL/Python scripts in the terminal to do the whole task.

integrity.description: The calculated coordinates are genuine spatial data, not manually typed dummy values
integrity.visual_evidence: The X and Y columns in the attribute table or CSV contain realistic longitude (approx -122.x) and latitude (approx 37.x) values, not placeholder text or manually typed integers.

integrity.description: The CSV was generated via QGIS export, not manually typed in a text editor
integrity.visual_evidence: The CSV file contains exactly 10 rows of data with precise floating-point coordinates and matching attributes, which would be practically impossible to manually type out accurately without using the QGIS export function.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output CSV path is /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/polygon_vertices.csv. The QGIS project must be saved to /home/ga/GIS_Data/projects/vertex_extraction.qgs. The input data contains 2 polygons with 5 vertices each (4 corners + 1 closing vertex), so the resulting point layer and CSV should contain exactly 10 rows/features. The coordinates are in WGS84 (EPSG:4326), with Longitude (X) values in the range [-122.5, -121.9] and Latitude (Y) values in the range [37.5, 37.8].
